You can now trade polymarket with their capital instead of your own

@FundingPredicts is a prop firm built only for prediction markets, here is how it actually works

You pick an account size from 10k up to 150k, pay a one time challenge fee between 85 and 665, then hit a profit target while staying inside the drawdown

The 50k is the popular one, 260 fee, 3,000 to clear, 1,500 trailing drawdown, 1,000 daily loss cap, single phase, 30 days on the clock

Pass it and you get funded on the same live Polymarket orderbook, no simulated fills, real events across politics, crypto, macro and sports

Split starts at 80% and unlocks to 90%, payouts hit every 7 days, funds in your hands within 72 hours

The backing is the part most people skip over, they are funded by myfundedfutures, 180m paid to traders across 114k payouts since 2023

Honest take, the fee is real and the drawdown is tight, so this only works if you already carry edge into prediction markets

Dear @blknoiz06

Down to my last $4.88 and somehow the coin with your name on it is the only bag up 621% 😭

So is this the universe telling you to carry the trenches again or nah

Job not Finished until 1B https://t.co/IHn78HTOPz

A kid in the early 90s obsessed with gaming and combat sports

- Studies computer science at georgia tech
- Works as a software engineer after graduating
- Stumbles into bitcoin during college, then goes all in around 2017

2017 to 2021:

- Enters crypto with just $3,000 and a lot of conviction
- Grinds through cycles that made most people quit
- Goes full time on trading by 2021

The solana bet:

- Buys sol near $1.50 when almost nobody believed in it
- Rides the price past $200 as the ecosystem explodes
- Becomes one of the loudest solana voices after ftx collapses

2023 to 2024:

- Calls dogwifhat at a $100k market cap
- Watches that cap run toward billions at its peak
- Backs bonk for another 80x and earns the memecoin king nickname

Beyond trading:

- Becomes head of research at tcg crypto, a chernin group arm
- Takes early venture positions in monad, solayer and ethena
- Builds bullpenfi, a home for high risk perps degens

The other side of the story:

- Zachxbt accuses him of turning his followers into exit liquidity
- Plenty of his low cap calls bleed to zero
- He fires back that meme coins are simply what retail wants

The boxing chapter:

- Steps into the ring at crypto fight night against bitboy
- The bout ends in a draw

Now:

- Net worth lands somewhere between $20 and $30 million
- More than 1M followers wait on his next call
- Holding 60% supply for token solana:9cRCn9rGT8V2imeM2BaKs13yhMEais3ruM3rPvTGpump 
- Still hunting the next thousand x runner

Ansem

Born June 28 1971 in Pretoria, South Africa

- Grows up in a wealthy but turbulent family
- Teaches himself to code on a Commodore VIC-20
- At age 12 builds a video game called Blastar and sells it to a magazine for around $500

Late 80s:

- Leaves South Africa at 17 to dodge mandatory military service
- Moves to Canada in 1989 on his mother's citizenship

College:

- Studies at Queen's University in Canada
- Transfers to the University of Pennsylvania
- Earns degrees in physics and economics

1995:

- Moves to California for a Stanford PhD
- Drops out after two days
- Co-founds Zip2 with his brother Kimbal

1999:

- Compaq buys Zip2 for around $307 million
- He walks away with about $22 million
- Co-founds https://t.co/Xe60j5Cu4C, an online bank

2000 to 2002:

- https://t.co/Xe60j5Cu4C merges with Confinity and becomes PayPal
- eBay buys PayPal for $1.5 billion
- His cut as largest shareholder is around $180 million

2002:

- Founds SpaceX with one goal, get humans to Mars

2004:

- Leads Tesla's first funding round
- Becomes chairman, then CEO

2008:

- Both Tesla and SpaceX nearly go bankrupt
- Falcon 1 reaches orbit, the first private liquid-fueled rocket to do it
- A NASA contract saves the whole thing at the last moment

2012 to 2017:

- SpaceX Dragon becomes the first commercial craft to dock with the ISS
- Co-founds OpenAI, launches Starlink, founds Neuralink and The Boring Company

2020 to 2021:

- Crew Dragon flies NASA astronauts, the first private crewed orbital flight
- Becomes the richest person on earth
- Tesla crosses a $1 trillion market cap

2022 to 2023:

- Buys Twitter for $44 billion and rebrands it to X
- Founds xAI and builds Grok

2024 to 2025:

- Becomes the biggest donor in the US election, backing Trump
- Leads DOGE, then exits in May 2025
- Weeks later a public feud with Trump blows up across X and Truth Social

November 2025:

- Tesla approves a pay package worth up to $1 trillion tied to milestones

June 12 2026:

- SpaceX goes public on Nasdaq as SPCX
- Largest IPO in history, around $75 billion raised, near $1.77 trillion valuation
- He becomes the first trillionaire the world has ever seen

Now:

- Net worth peaks near $1.32 trillion, then drops about $240 billion in a single week
- Still the only trillionaire alive
- Turns 55 today

Full circle:

- Started by selling a $500 game as a kid
- Now owns the most valuable rocket company ever built

Elon Musk

Everyone wants the blue badge before they have a single win on record

This account skipped all of it

Joined june, sitting at 0 views, 2 predictions deep, biggest win still blank, 30 dollars total on the line

Nobody watching, no track record worth defending, just one quiet position riding unnoticed

And this is exactly the wallet shape catching polymarket runs before clout farmers flood in

So be honest, brand new 30 dollar account, dead money you scroll past or early before anyone looks

RT @bryantheden: Everyone screaming Polymarket launch $POLY now while the world cup prints, that's exactly why they shouldn't

World cup wi…

Ansem just handed alon the entire bull case for shipping the pump airdrop early, and almost nobody read it right

His point is dead simple, even if the airdrop plus every unlock got maximally dumped, barely 250m hits the most active degens onchain, and money like that does not exit, it gets blasted straight back into coins

First look at where pump actually sits, 533m mcap, price flatlined near 0.0013, parked on top of a 382m buyback that already torched 36 percent of supply, the machine works, fuel tank just runs empty

Next look at solana, dex volume ripped 39 in a month to 1.73b a day, yet meme share cratered under 10 from the 70 it ran in 2024, stablecoins are now 80 of the chain, casino doors open but gamblers went broke

And now the july 12 unlock, another 82.5b tokens worth 108m hits market, 65m straight to team, 42m to early backers, pure sell pressure with zero fresh buyers walking through

This is exactly why @blknoiz06 keeps pushing @a1lon9 to ship sooner, that airdrop is not a gift, it is reload ammo for every trader pump rinsed across a full year, hand rekt degens their refund and watch it loop right back into volume, fees, burns

So which one is it, the spark that finally lights solana summer, or just the last bag insiders need filled before july 12 dumps

Everyone keeps tagging @world_xyz thinking it's sam altman's worldcoin

It's not even the same universe

This one is a stealth solana project that dropped 80k on a domain just to whisper two words, trade everything

Still in coming soon mode since 2023, zero product, full mystery

Then the certificate logs slipped

A live console just surfaced at https://t.co/zYzg1uKK65, sitting behind a password gate

Fresh cert, brand new infra, not some old test page

Nobody spins up an lp portal for an identity app, only a trading venue onboarding market makers does that

Every perp dex ran this exact playbook right before going live

So which is it @world_xyz, the next hyperliquid killer or another stealth tease that ghosts us in 6 months

Drop the password or let ct keep digging

The oracle @Polymarket trusts to settle a 60m market is worth 37m total

UMA protocol just printed the lowest price in its entire history, 35 cents on june 6

Down 99% from its peak, a 37m cap token decides who wins polymarket bets bigger than itself

Meanwhile strategy, the company this whole fight is about, sits down 75% on its year at a 23 month low

So here is the real question

Should a token at all time lows be the final call on millions flowing through polymarket, or is this oracle model already broken

Everyone screaming Polymarket launch $POLY now while the world cup prints, that's exactly why they shouldn't

World cup winner contract alone ripped 3.1b of volume, soccer blew past 2b within ten days, daily sports flow jumped from 53m to 220m, the crowd loud and undeniably real

And now the part nobody clips, that volume is a sugar high, cup final resolves july 19, bid leaves when whistle blows

Real tell isn't a spike, it's polymarket crossing 1b annualized revenue this week, that number is the floor, rest stays noise

Their own cmo praised hyperliquid for launching with zero hype and lasting forever, so firing $POLY to milk a sports pop would betray the exact playbook they bragged about

So here's the only question worth a reply, drop $POLY now and ride attention you can't keep, or hold till final whistle and prove that floor is real, which side you fading

Polymarket has 200+ builders routing billions through open onchain codes

Kalshi has zero public builders and still owns 52 percent of all volume

everyone reads that as Kalshi being more real
wrong, the gap is not activity it is architecture

one is a walled garden with a 23M account robinhood pipe and 80 percent sports churn, the other is permissionless so you can audit every single dollar onchain

now the part nobody admits, that builder board overstates flow 2x and six apps control 81 percent of it

so tell me which number is actually honest
the open volume you can verify or the closed volume you just have to trust

Pump made over $1,000,000,000 in revenue and gave the people who built it a $0 airdrop

Read that again

The launchpad that printed the entire meme meta, 12 million+ tokens, 200k to 300k daily traders, every fee on that machine paid by degens, and their reward for all of it was nothing, no snapshot, no retro, no drop

Instead $PUMP raised a billion dollars across its ICO and private rounds, if you wanted in you paid for it, the community that generated the volume got a buy button not a thank you

Now flip to Hyperliquid

31% of supply sent straight to 94,000 real users, zero VC, zero exchange allocation, the largest airdrop in crypto history, 1,589 wallets turned into millionaires on day one

Today $HYPE sits top 10 near a $16B cap, 99% of fees buying the token back for the people who hold it, that is what respecting a community actually looks like

The pattern is not subtle, the projects that pay their users forward win, the ones that extract from them slowly bleed the meta dry

So here is the take nobody on CT wants to post

Memes do not come back from another buyback chart, they come back the day pump turns around and rewards the people who made the $1B, the revenue is there, the users are there, 50% of every fee already locked to buyback and burn, the only missing piece is the drop

And the guy already running this exact playbook is @blknoiz06, airdropping his own creator fees back to his followers, that is the model, that is the signal, scale it to the whole platform and the meta wakes up overnight

Real question, does $PUMP need a retroactive airdrop to revive memes, or is the buyback alone enough

would love your read on this @blknoiz06
RT @blknoiz06: there's enough good tokens that exist already, but sure i will airdrop portions of the creator fees that have been directed…

So tell me where your conviction sits or admit the naming was never an accident
